title: chenopodium album subsp. borbasii
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. borbasii, also known as lamb's quarters, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium album plant, which is a type of annual herbaceous plant in the goosefoot family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antioxidant. The health benefits of lamb's quarters include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of lamb's quarters can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
